title: 8 Processing Business Tax Payments and Filings Electronically
body_text:
8	Processing Business Tax Payments and Filings Electronically
8.1	Overview of Processing Business Tax Payments and Filings Electronically
Most CRA business tax payments and filings can be submitted to CRA via Central 1âs Business Taxes. The procedures in this chapter apply to front-line staff in financial institutions that practice the single workflow and to back-office staff in financial institutions that practice the combined workflow. See Chapter 4, Workflow Options for Business Taxes, for more information.
Note:	See Bank Truncation Guide for CRA Remittance Vouchers, Form 10138, for the lists of  CRA Payees that can be electronically remitted.
Important
:	Corporate and personal CRA Billers donât accept same-day bill payment reversal requests. The complete list of CRA billers that donât accept same-day bill payment reversal requests is available
here
. As payments to these Biller types canât be reversed, non-same-day traces can be submitted via the Online Tracing System (OLT). See the Online Tracing System Part for more information on how to create a trace request.
8.2	Submitting Files and Tax Payments to CRA via Business Taxes
Proceed as follows to submit a file or a tax payment in Business Taxes:
Verify that the remittance voucher is stamped as required.
Log in to Business Taxes, as described in Section 7.3, Logging in to the Production Environment.
Choose the appropriate branch from the âSelect a branchâ field. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select a Branch
Choose the applicable form from the âSelect Tax Type/Programâ drop-down list. Alternatively, start typing the form name or number to filter the results. See Figure 2.
Note:	Form numbers may include an âEâ or âFâ character. This is the language code for âEnglishâ or âFrenchâ. Donât include this language character when searching for a form.
Note:	If the electronic form isnât listed in Business Taxes, the remittance voucher must be sent to CRA by mail or by courier. See Chapter 9, Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually.
Figure 2: Selecting a Tax Form
Complete the required fields on the electronic form with the information provided on the CRA remittance voucher. See Figure 3.
Important
:	The filing date for business tax payments can be backdated five business days if your financial institution is using the combined workflow of front-line and back-office staff. The filing date canât be forward-dated. Payments received during extended business hours must be processed the next business day and date-stamped accordingly.

Figure 3: Example of a Business Tax Form

Figure 5: Verifying Tax Remittance Information

8.3	Viewing Archived CRA Transactions
CRA Business Tax transactions are stored in
PaymentStream
â¢ Direct for 18 months. The
PaymentStream
Direct archive provides transaction details for submitted tasks. Tasks are only created for transactions that involve the movement of money.
To view archived tasks, proceed as follows:
Log on to
PaymentStream
Direct as described in Section 7.3, Logging in to the Production Environment.
Select the
Administration
tab. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Accessing Administration in PaymentStream Direct
Enter search criteria to find the appropriate archived transaction. Itâs not necessary to complete all the fields. See Figure 8.
â
Enter the transit number where the remittance was made.
â¡
Enter the initiator ID.
â¢
Select below, above, or equal from the drop-down list.
â£
Enter an amount.
â¤
Enter an initiated date range.
â¥
Select
Search
. The results are displayed at the bottom of the page.
â¦
Sort results by Initiated Date.
â§
Sort results by Amount.
â¨
Select the
CSV
icon to export the list of archived transactions to Excel.
â©
Select the link in the Status column to view the archived transaction.
Figure 8: Entering Search Criteria
The Business Taxes page shows the details of the archived transaction. See Figure 9.
Right-click on the page and select
Print
to print the information.
Select
Return to Tasks
to go back to the Archived Tasks search page.
Figure 9: Business Taxes â Archived Transaction Details
